task: I need to send a short follow-up to a customer after a delayed update, but I don’t want it to sound stiff or overdone. Can you draft something I could use, and keep it to a couple of sentences?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple version you could use:

“Just following up with a quick update: the storm has slowed things down, so the order is still behind schedule. I can’t confirm the next movement yet, but I’ll send the next confirmed update by [time/date].”

If you want, I can make it sound a little warmer or more direct.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but a little too stiff still. I’d want it more natural, like:

“Quick update on your order — the weather delay is still pushing things back, but I’m checking on it and will send you the next confirmed status by [time/date]. I’ll keep you posted as soon as I have anything solid.”

If you want, give me the tone you want — more formal, more friendly, or more firm — and I can tighten it up.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more natural version:

“Quick update on your order: the weather is still causing delays, but I’m checking on it and will send the next confirmed status by [time/date]. I’ll let you know as soon as I have something solid.”

If you want, I can make it even more casual or a little more customer-friendly.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Natural enough, not overdone.
